title: The 'Kitchen Sink' Le Creuset Frittata

The Fillings: 1 cup coleslaw mix, cabbage and carrots
1/2 cup carrots, grated or finely shredded
1/2 cup snow peas, sliced diagonally into thirds
3 units green onions, whites diced and greens reserved for garnish

steps: Verify the freshness of 9 eggs using a float test, then whisk them with heavy cream, salt, pepper, and half of the cheese until the mixture is frothy.
Heat oil in a Le Creuset 30 over medium-high heat and sauté the mushrooms until they release moisture and turn golden brown.
Add the bell pepper, carrots, coleslaw mix, and green onion whites to the pan; sauté for 4–5 minutes until the cabbage is wilted.
Stir in the garlic, snow peas, cherry tomatoes, and olives, cooking for about 2 minutes until the tomatoes just begin to soften.
Level the vegetables across the pan, lower the heat to medium-low, and pour the egg mixture over the top, letting it set on the stovetop for 3–4 minutes.
Sprinkle the remaining cheese over the surface and bake in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven for 8–10 minutes until the center is firm.
